claimText: An earthquake swarm is a sequence of multiple earthquakes occurring in a short timeframe without a distinct main shock.
evidenceExcerpt: Earthquake Swarm: Sequences of many earthquakes that occur in a relatively short period without a specific main shock.
examUse: Statement-based question distinguishing earthquake swarms from typical mainshock-aftershock sequences.
distractorHooks: Occurs over a prolonged geological period

claimText: Swell waves, also referred to as Kallakkadal waves in India, are long-wavelength ocean waves that remain largely unaffected by local winds.
evidenceExcerpt: Swell Waves: Long-wavelength ocean waves travelling long distances away from their origin. Largely unaffected by local winds and often go undetected by satellites... Also called Kallakkadal waves in India.
examUse: Correct-pair question matching local terminology (Kallakkadal) to oceanographic phenomena.

claimText: A blowout is an industrial accident in an oil or gas well caused by underground pressure forcing hydrocarbons to the surface uncontrollably.
evidenceExcerpt: Blowout: Oil/gas well accident where underground pressure forces gas/oil to surface uncontrollably.
examUse: Statement-based question on the causes of oil and gas well disasters.
distractorHooks: Surface-level pipeline rupture due to external impact

claimText: A Mesoscale Convective System (MCS) is a large collection of thunderstorms that act together as a system and can persist for over 12 hours.
evidenceExcerpt: Mesoscale Convective System (MCS): Collection of thunderstorms that act as a system. Can spread across entire state and last >12 hours.
examUse: Statement-based question on the characteristics and duration of mesoscale weather events.
distractorHooks: Lasts for less than an hour
